screens.name: Morning dispatch board
screens.purpose: Help a supervisor turn seasonal intent and live field signals into executable work while distinguishing blocked, partial, offline, corrected, completed, and verified records without flattening history.
screens.primary_decision: Which work proceeds today, which plan inputs require intervention, and which blocked or completed records need resolution?

states.description: The work plan is authorized before it can be assigned.
Work is owned with current scope, timing, resources, and closure evidence.
The foreman confirmed the current assignment and field constraints.
The foreman is executing the acknowledged assignment with attributed actuals.
Execution stopped with reason, affected scope, and accountable reporter.
Safe completed acreage is retained while remaining rows stay open.
A queued field event is retained without overwriting a newer authoritative server state.
Execution is finished and awaits accountable verification.
A supervisor confirmed final acreage and preserved exception history.
Past transitions remain attributable and immutable.

screens.permission_behavior: Foremen acknowledge assignments, execute work, and report partial or completed scope; operations coordinators assign and redispatch; vineyard managers contain blocked work and verify closure.
screens.offline_behavior: Field transitions queue with local timestamps; a canonical in-progress to partial event is retained as an immutable note when the server state advanced first.
screens.validation_behavior: Verification is rejected when completed acreage exceeds block acreage or blocker resolution evidence is absent.
